human_verification.notes: Reviewed validated readiness for aerospace-and-defense engineering change coordination specificity, source quality, configuration-control and handoff discipline, traceability and release-boundary protection, commercialization completeness, and explicit specification-based runtime positioning.

review_notes: All required shared, workflow phase, and manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities are represented and validated.
All 12 required scenarios are present and pass.
Research-summary records no blocking unresolved ambiguity; remaining ambiguity is explicitly tenant, customer, jurisdiction, program, or retrieval work.
No retained implementation is claimed in this specification-based package.

specialist_owned_evidence: Scope is materially distinct from generic coordination through aerospace-and-defense ECO, ECR, ECP, NOC, CCB, MRB, configuration baseline, effectivity, supplier, quality hold, FAI, source inspection, production, inventory, and logistics artifacts.

Boundary handling explicitly blocks engineering approval, CCB decisions, MRB disposition, final quality release, supplier substitution approval, supplier change approval, airworthiness signoff, legal interpretation, export-control determination, classified-program handling decisions, and direct execution work.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope coordination, missing prerequisites, duplicate ownership, traceability conflict, quality holds, supplier change pressure, low-confidence handling, policy conflict, tenant adaptation, terminology normalization, closure confirmation, and supplier nonconformance or safety-release escalation.
Promotion to a retained implementation is explicitly deferred to a separate governance and build decision.
notes: validated packaging completed for aerospace-and-defense engineering change coordination.
